0.response: {"Q0":"The only strategy I used was when the astronaut left the blue planet, I would say to myself after every round: \"only red planet\" and keep repeating that phrase. "}

7.response: {"Q0":"I tried to pick the galaxy that had obvious choices of which outcome was better. When it was hard to decide or felt like time was running out, I made a random choice. "}

9.response: {"Q0":"I was unsure if the astronaut came back and was \"reset\" after the round in which he left."}

1.response: {"Q0":"No, I did not change my strategy as I had no reason to because the only planet that had an astronaut leave was the blue planet. "}

5.response: {"Q0":"No, I did not keep digging from the planet which he left. "}

2.response: {"Q0":"I feel like the frequency of finding gems on the red and blue planets was about 20%-30%. I feel like most of the things I dug up were rocks and only a few gems. "}

6.response: {"Q0":"No, I did not collect the gems from the relevant color that the astronaut left. "}

8.response: {"Q0":"I was more focused on digging at the right colored planet because it was an easier task compared to trying to select the better galaxy. "}

3.response: {"Q0":"Yes, when I received the message about the astronaut leaving the blue planet, I kept a mental note to only dig up the red planets. "}

4.response: {"Q0":"Yes the implications were clear to me. However, I was unsure if the game \"reset\" after the first round of the astronaut leaving. I wasn't sure if the astronaut was still gone or not but I figured it out after the second round of him being gone. "}

11.response: {"Q0":"I enjoyed taking this study. Thank you!"}
